items.title: After 18 months of algal bloom, bird flu has authorities on alert

children.content: While the arrival of bird flu on SA shores is a "huge concern", the state's primary industries minister maintains the government is prepared for the possibility of a wider local outbreak.

items.title: SA police security officer threatened partner with gun, court hears

children.content: A South Australian police security officer stood trial in South Australia's District Court this week, accused of threatening his partner with a police-issued firearm.

image.altText: Man in blue button up shirt and blue reflective sunglasses walks surrounded by media

items.title: Curnow hits front in Coleman Medal race as Swans and Crows record wins

children.content: Charlie Curnow kicks six goals as the Swans cruise to a 35-point win over the Bulldogs, while Adelaide holds off a fast-finishing West Coast in Perth.

image.altText: Two AFL players in red and white celebrate a goal together

items.title: Renewables company ZEN Energy enters voluntary administration

children.content: ZEN Energy was once an emerging player in the nation's renewables transition but has now entered voluntary administration, saying its viability had been impacted by "wholesale electricity volatility".

gallery.altText: Powerlines suspended between towers against orange dusk  sky.

thumbnail.altText: Powerlines suspended between towers against orange dusk  sky.

items.title: SA music festival Harvest Rock going on hiatus

children.content: Promoter Secret Sounds has announced that the SA government-backed event won't be going ahead this October.

image.altText: A crowd of people in front of a big festival stage on a clear day

items.title: Man rescued from flood waters as SA wild weather leaves trail of damage

children.content: Storm damage have prompted extensive clean-ups across South Australia while a man had to be rescued from his vehicle's rooftop after he tried to drive through flood waters.

image.altText: SES volunteers in orange suits gather around white emergency ahead of the rescue of a man stranded in floodwater.

items.title: Arson attacks and shooting linked with organised crime, SA police say

children.content: Detectives say they believe arson attacks on a car wash and another on an office block in Adelaide are linked with a shooting in the city's east in February.

items.title: Tree protester warned not to reoffend says she would 'absolutely' do it again

children.content: Activist and author Mij Tanith refuses to rule out being arrested again for protesting against the felling of trees in the Adelaide Park Lands, despite a warning from a magistrate she could face a conviction next time.

image.altText: A woman with short grey hair wearing a purple jacket speaks to microphones while other people hold signs behind

items.title: Super Netball's best buy almost signed for grand final rival

children.content: South African import Elmeré van der Berg has scored more goals than anyone else in her debut Super Netball season. Ahead of the grand final she speaks about her 'resting bitch face', retraining her brain to embrace weight gain, and the tweaks the Adelaide Thunderbirds have made to take her game to another level.

image.altText: A player holds the ball and looks up to goal

items.title: Remembering 'Miss Stayput's' legendary stand against SA's largest shopping mall

children.content: Eva Phelps was 82 when she refused to sell her land for the development of Marion Shopping Centre, so it was built around her, making her small house a veritable castle.

image.altText: An office building with Westfield written at the top

items.title: MP calls for investigation into anti-abortion campaigner

children.content: Anti-abortion campaigner Joanna Howe has been called out for potentially breaching campaign financing laws, with one MP labelling her "attack" style as harmful and dangerous.

image.altText: Joanna Howe stands next to women holding up ban feticide sign.

items.title: Governments warned about $240m Nyrstar subsidy payouts

children.content: A group of international shareholders are warning state and federal governments against giving global commodities giant Trafigura hundreds of millions of dollars to keep smelting operations going in Australia.

items.title: Explosion in feral pig numbers attributed to major flooding in far north SA

children.content: Feral pig numbers are exploding in outback South Australia after record-breaking floods, prompting a call from station owners and the Invasive Species Council for governments to take action.

image.altText: A mob of feral pigs wading across and out of flood waters

items.title: Fans hope to see Thunderbirds win netball grand final for third time

children.content: Among the devoted Thunderbirds fans making the journey to Melbourne for the grand final against the Vixens are the Rawlins family, including sisters Tess and Eve.

avatar.alt: A headshot of a smiling woman with a dark background

items.title: School worker sexually assaulted girls in Adelaide Hills, court told

children.content: A contract worker accused  of indecently assaulted two students at an Adelaide Hills school he worked at has appeared in court for the first time.

image.altText: A sign that says "Mount Barker Courthouse" stands outside a brick building.

items.title: Arsonist with 'fixated discontent' jailed over $1.5m hotel fire

children.content: A judge has jailed a man who "embarked on a consistent campaign of terror" against his former business partner, including torching the complex containing the Oxford Hotel in North Adelaide.

items.title: Major milk company fined for misleading consumers about fresh milk

children.content: One of Australia's largest milk providers has been fined by the consumer watchdog for misleading consumers with "fresh milk" labels on products that contained substantial amounts of powdered milk.

image.altText: Milk pouring into a cup with soft green background

items.title: Ange Postecoglou appointed head coach at Saudi club Al-Nassr

children.content: The 60-year-old takes over the Riyadh-based side led by Portugal forward Cristiano Ronaldo.

image.altText: A man looks out onto a football field.

items.title: Man who spent eight days stuck under collapsed shopping centre in Venezuela rescued alive

children.content: Hernan Alberto Gil Flores, a 43-year-old Venezuelan man was dug out alive from under the ruins of a collapsed shopping centre, eight days after devastating earthquakes shook the nation.

image.altText: Rescue workers and group of people surround ambulance.

gallery.altText: a man on a stretcher with no shirt and an oxygen mask on

thumbnail.altText: a man on a stretcher with no shirt and an oxygen mask on

items.title: Billionaires to every day people: Inquiry exposes extent of online hate

children.content: Everyone from billionaires to politicians and pro-Palestinian Jewish activists told the royal commission of the hate they receive in phone calls and emails.

image.altText: composite image of Julie Inman Grant, steven lowy and sarah Schwartz

items.title: Today Show ratings in 'freefall' as Channel Nine looks to replace Karl Stefanovic

children.content: The Today Show's ratings are in decline following the Nine Network's dumping of host Karl Stefanovic. Former head of News and Current Affairs at Seven, Nine, and 10, Peter Meakin says the audience will want to punish Nine, but things can turn around, even in breakfast TV.

image.altText: Karl Stefanovic in a suit, no tie smiling and standing in front of the Crown

items.title: When a fit young man disappears, he leaves behind a trail of strange clues

children.content: When the first major relationship of Jack McLennan's life implodes, he does what plenty of young men do: meets a mate for a quiet beer. But by morning, Jack's disappeared, leaving behind a series of mysterious clues and a devastated family.

gallery.altText: kellie stands in front of house holding sign saying "bring jack home"
the water at sunset at Ficks crossing
Jack McLennan and Carlee Beetham pictured happy together in front of a waterfall

thumbnail.altText: kellie stands in front of house holding sign saying "bring jack home"
the water at sunset at Ficks crossing
Jack McLennan and Carlee Beetham pictured happy together in front of a waterfall

items.title: 'Towelling the bloke up in the cells': Shocking police emails revealed

children.content: Emails reveal an extraordinary exchange between AFP officers after an Indigenous man was allegedly beaten and illegally strip-searched at a Canberra watchhouse.

image.altText: A person's face and police tape and bars in a graphic image.

items.title: Kasatkina bows out of Wimbledon as Osaka stuns on and off court

children.content: Daria Kasatkina bows out of Wimbledon to end Australia's run in the women's draw.

items.title: Hugh Grant, Benson Boone among stars at rumoured Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding

children.content: A heavy media presence and barricades surround the Madison Square Garden stadium where fans wait in sweltering conditions.

gallery.altText: Hugh Grant and partner in a red dress walk down street.
A man with sunnies and a suit.
Two men in blac walk past barricades.

thumbnail.altText: Hugh Grant and partner in a red dress walk down street.
A man with sunnies and a suit.
Two men in blac walk past barricades.
